plan_metadata.name: Sample Tab With Azure Backend Remote Debug

steps.description:
Click the "Create" button for the "Tab App with Azure Backend" sample in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit Samples panel within Visual Studio Code.
And input ${{var:app_name:tab-with-backend}} into the run time variable.
Click the "Deploy" button under the "LIFECYCLE" section in the MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT panel within Visual Studio Code to execute the deploy lifecycle stage.
Click the "Deploy" button in the confirmation dialog that asks "Do you want to deploy resources in dev environment?" within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "y" key prompt in the terminal of Visual Studio Code to confirm proceeding with the installation of @azure/static-web-apps-cli.
Press Enter key in the VS Code terminal to confirm the prompt "Ok to proceed? (y/n)" during Azure Static Web Apps deployment using the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.
@assertion there's notification: "actions in deploy stage executed successfully." exist
Click the "Build your app locally" link in the main content area of the Microsoft Teams web app under the "tab-with-backenddev" tab.
Scroll to the bottom of the "tab-with-backenddev" tab content in Microsoft Teams web interface by pressing the End key.
Click the “Authorize and call Azure Function” button in the Microsoft Teams web app under the "tab-with-backenddev" tab.
Click the "Accept" button on the Microsoft permissions request dialog to grant requested permissions to the application.
@assertion there's message: "User display name is " exist.
